title: Signal Contamination

content.statement: Once people optimize the metric, the metric stops cleanly representing reality.
content.question: Which business metric are you about to start optimizing that could get gamed or distorted?
content.equation: SC = (Gaming + Proxy + Bias + Lag + Omission) ÷ Total Metrics
sop: List your top 5 tracked metrics and, for each, ask how someone could hit this number without actually helping the business.
Flag any metric where you already suspect gaming, sandbagging, or distortion.
Pair each vanity-prone metric with a context metric that would catch the gaming (e.g., pair leads with close rate).
Rate Gaming, Proxy, Bias, Lag, and Omission risk 1-10 for your top metric, sum them, and divide by your Total Metrics tracked (see calculation, page 6)
Rotate or re-define your top metrics every 6-12 months before they fully stop meaning what you think they mean.
content.explainer: Every metric has a half-life before it starts lying to you. Rotate them or add context metrics.
questions: Which business metric are you about to start optimizing that could get gamed or distorted?
Which of your dashboard numbers hasn't been double-checked against reality in the last quarter?
If your team optimized purely for your top-line metric, what corner would they cut first?

definitions.def: Deliberately lowballing a target or hiding capacity so you can easily beat it and look good. A common way people game the numbers they're measured on.

content.equation_example: Rate Gaming, Proxy, Bias, Lag, and Omission risk 1-10 each for your top metric, sum them, then divide by your Total Metrics tracked. Gaming 6, Proxy 5, Bias 4, Lag 3, Omission 2, sum 20, Total Metrics tracked 5 → SC = 20 ÷ 5 = 4. Higher SC means your metrics are more likely lying to you; lower means your numbers still reflect reality.
content.closing_statement: Every metric has a shelf life. Watch for it.

caption:
Principle Nº 27: Signal Contamination

Once people optimize the metric, the metric stops cleanly representing reality. Every metric has a half-life before it starts lying to you.

Ask yourself: Which business metric are you about to start optimizing that could get gamed or distorted?
